title: Pedestrian Footbridge Active Tuned Mass Damper Controller
description: Detects footbridge vertical vibration via MPU-6050 accelerometer, computes vibration RMS over a sliding window using vibration_rms_window, and drives a DC motor-actuated 500 g mass carriage in anti-phase to cancel resonant excitation at 1.8 Hz — an active strategy distinct from passive damper projects, targeting pedestrian lock-step crowd loading.
objective: Reduce peak vertical acceleration from >0.5 m/s² to <0.1 m/s² (ISO 10137 comfort limit) using active TMD; alarm and motor disable at carriage end-stop.
constraints: Arduino Mega, MPU-6050 IMU, L298N H-bridge, 500 g carriage, 160 mm rail, linear potentiometer, buzzer; bridge span ≤ 15 m, fn = 1.5–2.5 Hz; 12 V supply.
supporting_fact_titles: Structural Resonance and Dynamic Amplification Factor
Tuned Mass Damper Optimal Frequency and Damping Ratios
design_element_titles: DC Motor Driven Tuned Mass Carriage
Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller

element_usage_notes.DC Motor Driven Tuned Mass Carriage: Primary actuator; driven in anti-phase to bridge acceleration for active cancellation.
element_usage_notes.Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controlle: Inner loop: carriage velocity from potentiometer derivative; outer loop: bridge acceleration error vs. 0.05 m/s² setpoint.
element_usage_notes.Buzzer and LED Fault Annunciator: Comfort alarm at RMS >0.5 m/s²; safety disable at carriage end-stop contact.
element_usage_notes.HX711 Load Cell Amplifier Array: Measures rail end-stop contact force to prevent mechanical over-travel damage.

element_interactions.to: DC Motor Driven Tuned Mass Carriage
element_interactions.description: End-stop force >5 N forces motor off to prevent carriage over-travel.

element_interactions.from: DC Motor Driven Tuned Mass Carriage
element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: Carriage position derivative gives velocity for inner PID loop.

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: RMS acceleration above comfort and alarm thresholds drives annunciator outputs.
